What I'm going to be doing this morning is I'm going to be doing a couple things. So number one is I'm going to be talking to you about the ten big lessons that I've learned from 33 years in the coaching and consulting business, which in and of itself is kind of like scary that I've been doing this thing for 33 years. And I'll tell you about that in just a second. So then we're going to spend about 15 minutes or so at the end of the hour that I'm spending with you today. If anybody wants to come up to the table, one of the things that Dan asked me to discuss with you is any kind of structure questions. So how do you structure things, specifically group coaching sessions? So if you have any questions about structuring that, I know a lot of people in the room here are in either my Info Mastermind group or my VIP group. So you kind of see how I do a lot of the structuring already. And those of you who have been in Dan's Platinum group or VIP groups in the past or VIP millionaire or whatever else he's had, you've seen how he's done it. The structuring of mine and the structuring of his are very similar, in fact. So just to let you know a little bit about my 33 years, in 1974 is when I first went into the consulting business, and actually as no surprise to all of you in the room, my initial consulting was in retail. And my expertise in 1974, believe it or not, was I worked with retailers who were liquidating their business. So I was the hired gun that was coming in and worked in their businesses to help them to liquidate their businesses. And I did this through all the 70s and also through the 80s, and I grew the largest retail liquidation company in the Mid-Atlantic area. And it was really the lion's share of my revenue back then, more so than my own retail business was. And I was the guy that came in and put up all the orange signs that you see on the windows and all that other stuff. And I actually also turned it into a course where I had a seven-step system for liquidating a retail business, and I would have people come in and they would hire me on different levels. One level was we'd come in and do the wholesale for them. Another level they would do, they could hire me on the lowest level, which is for two hours, they could come in and I would teach them how to do it themselves over two hours. And so I did that through the 70s and the 80s. In 1999, after having lunch with Dan in 1998, and having this lunch where I thanked him for helping me grow my retail business, and he was the one that said to me at that time, you know, Bill, you're doing such great stuff in your retail business, you should be teaching this to other menswear retailers. In 1999, I started my BGS marketing system. And with the BGS marketing system, I provided then and still provide today a very painful one-on-one coaching for an hour. If you invest in the BGS marketing system, you get a certificate with me for one-on-one for an hour, which is very painful. And thank God very few people take advantage of those certificates. But I do that. I also sell coaching through BGS marketing. And the way that I sell coaching through BGS marketing is I do it on what I call a two-hour and minimal, which is we have a questionnaire that we... When somebody requests a coaching call with me, we send them out a questionnaire and they fill out the questionnaire, which is the questionnaire that I've developed, which they tell me all about their business, and they tell me about their specific marketing strategies, and they send it to me, and I review that for up to an hour, so that's the first of the two hours, then I get on the phone with them and I discuss it with them for the other hour, so that's the two hours. I charge $1,000 an hour, it's $2,000 minimal in order for me to do that for people. What that does is a couple of things. Number one is when I get on the phone with them, they don't spend a whole lot of time with me getting me up to speed, and usually that getting me up to speed is very painful because they don't know how to tell me things. So the other thing it does, which is the second thing, is it prepares them to how to deliver information to me. So that's how I do that. It's a two-hour process, one hour I review it, one hour on the phone with them, and that's $2,000.
